channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Suggests Ukraine War May Be Coming to an End
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said the war in Ukraine may be "coming to an end" as Ukraine recaptures territory and Russia faces mounting casualties and economic pressure. Ukraine has become a drone and missile power, exporting technology to Gulf states. - The Guardian
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: ⛪ Israeli Soldiers Jailed for Virgin Mary Statue Desecration in Lebanon
item.description: Two Israeli soldiers sentenced to military prison after a photo of one sticking a cigarette in a Virgin Mary statue went viral. The incident follows another where soldiers hacked a crucifix statue, drawing condemnation from Christian leaders. - The Guardian
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: 🇬🇧 Nearly 200 Sanctioned Russian Ships Entered UK Waters Despite Warning
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows 184 UK-sanctioned "shadow fleet" vessels made 238 journeys through UK waters since March, with no public evidence of boardings. Critics call lack of action "pathetic" amid legal constraints. - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Prime Minister Starmer on Brink as Cabinet Ministers Urge Resignation
item.description: Over 70 Labour MPs including cabinet ministers publicly called for Starmer to quit, with Yvette Cooper and Shabana Mahmood urging an orderly transition. Starmer refused to resign, saying the country would "never forgive" Labour for leadership chaos. - The Guardian
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran Ceasefire on Massive Life Support as Talks Falter
item.description: US President rejected Iran counter-offer as "totally unacceptable" and "garbage," calling Tehran leaders "dishonourable." Iran insists on ending Israel-Hezbollah fighting and lifting the US naval blockade of Iranian ports. - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇬🇱🇺🇸 US in Talks With Denmark to Open Three New Military Bases in Greenland
item.description: The US is negotiating with Denmark to establish three new military bases in southern Greenland, focusing on surveillance of Russian and Chinese activity in the Arctic. The talks aim to resolve diplomatic tensions after Trump's threat to seize the territory. - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇿🇦 South Africa President Ramaphosa Vows to Fight Impeachment, Won't Resign
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he will legally challenge a report paving the way for impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala cash theft scandal. The Constitutional Court ruled parliament must consider impeachment, but Ramaphosa declared "I remain here and am not resigning." - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 California Mayor Resigns, Pleads Guilty to Acting as Chinese Agent
item.description: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for China, resigning from her position. She was accused of promoting pro-PRC propaganda through a news website targeting Chinese Americans, including content denying Uyghur persecution. - AP News
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky's former chief of staff Yermak charged in major corruption probe
item.description: Andriy Yermak, who served as President Zelensky's second-in-command, has been charged in a major corruption investigation, marking a significant political development in Ukraine. - Kyiv Independent
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: ✈️🇪🇺 Ukraine turns to Europe for possible airport ceasefire with Russia
item.description: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha told Politico that Europe may help mediate an agreement between Ukraine and Russia to halt attacks on each other's airports. - Kyiv Independent
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇭🇺🇪🇺 Hungary vows to stop abusing EU veto rights, incoming foreign minister says
item.description: Hungary's incoming foreign minister Anita Orban stated that Hungary has too often used its veto "as political theater" and pledged to change course during a confirmation hearing. - Kyiv Independent
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🚢⚓ Epic Fury deployment has US Navy rethinking carrier deployment tempo
item.description: The record-breaking deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford has reopened debate about how the Navy should structure deployments and time at home for carrier strike groups. - Defense News
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🛢️💰 Morgan Stanley warns oil buffers could run out before Hormuz reopens
item.description: Most market buffers preventing oil futures from hitting record highs could vanish before the Strait of Hormuz reopens, creating potential for severe price spikes. - Oil Price
item.dc:creator: Oil Price

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK sanctions Iran-linked network for planning attacks
item.description: Britain froze assets and imposed travel bans on individuals accused of laundering billions and plotting Iranian-backed hostile activity on UK soil. - Al Jazeera
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU sanctions Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
item.description: The EU's foreign policy chief announced sanctions against violent Israeli settlers, saying "extremism and violence carry consequences." Israel called the move "arbitrary." - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Musk, Cook among 17 CEOs to join Trump on China visit
item.description: Top US executives including Elon Musk (Tesla/SpaceX), Tim Cook (Apple), and Larry Fink (BlackRock) will accompany President Trump to Beijing. The trip comes amid fragile tariff truce and Iranian war tensions affecting US-China relations. - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇺🇸 White House press dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, pleaded not guilty to federal gun charges and attempted assassination of President Trump after allegedly firing at a Secret Service agent during the White House Correspondents' dinner. - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine senator flees ICC arrest over Duterte drug war role
item.description: Senator Ronald Dela Rosa has evaded a warrant related to the drug war during Duterte presidency, fleeing abroad as ICC intensifies its investigation. - Al Jazeera
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK sanctions 85 people and entities over pro-Kremlin narratives
item.description: British government targets individuals and organizations accused of helping Russia forcibly deport and militarize Ukrainian children, calling it one of its "toughest measures" to date. - SCMP
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇰🇵 Russia paid North Korea up to $13.8B for war support
item.description: North Korea has earned billions for supplying weapons and soldiers to Russia's war in Ukraine, according to a new South Korean intelligence report. - Kyiv Independent
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: ⛽🇺🇸 Trump proposes suspending federal gasoline tax amid Iran war
item.description: President Trump announced plans to suspend the federal gasoline tax to ease prices as the Iran war continues to disrupt oil markets and keep the Strait of Hormuz closed. Critics note he cannot implement this unilaterally without Congress. - AP News
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🌏🛢️ Asia braces for second wave of energy shocks from Iran war
item.description: Asian economies are preparing for another wave of energy disruptions as the Iran conflict continues, with shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz remaining blocked and oil supplies constrained. - AP News
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇳🇬🇹🇩 Dozens of Nigerian Fishermen Feared Dead After Chad Air Strikes on Boko Haram
item.description: A fishermen's leader reports that 40 people are feared dead after Chad conducted air strikes on Boko Haram positions near Lake Chad, either from the strikes or drowning while fleeing. - BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷 UAE Secretly Carrying Out Attacks on Iran, WSJ Reports
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that the United Arab Emirates has been conducting covert attacks against Iran amid the ongoing conflict, marking a significant escalation of Gulf state involvement in the US-Iran confrontation. - Haaretz
item.dc:creator: Haaretz

item.title: 🇮🇱 Mossad Chief Warns Against Gofman Appointment in Letter to High Court
item.description: Outgoing Mossad chief sent a letter to Israel's High Court warning of "very great risk" from the appointment of Gofman, according to Haaretz. The warning comes amid significant security leadership transitions in Israel. - Haaretz
item.dc:creator: Haaretz
